# Build the founder's personal brand via IDEA
Maya runs a 7-figure DTC + Amazon **home goods** brand (organic cotton bedding + bath, hero SKU ASIN B0HOM0003) and is **prepping for an exit in ~12 months**. Her broker flagged "key-person/brand risk": the brand has no human face, so a buyer can't separate the IP from her, which caps the multiple. She wants to build a *transferable* founder personal brand — done-for-her personal UVP + a 90-day content plan — so the founder story becomes a documented, handover-able trust asset that lifts valuation, not a vanity project.

**Maya (User):** Exiting B0HOM0003 in ~12 months. Broker says my multiple's capped by "brand/key-person risk" — there's no founder story documented, so the trust looks like it lives in *me*, not the asset. I want to fix that: founder personal brand, LinkedIn-first, built so it transfers in diligence. I know IDEA, positioning, avatar — don't teach the pillars. Just give me a personal UVP I can use and a 90-day plan I can run (or hand to an operator). Background: 11 years as a hospital labor-and-delivery nurse, started this after the scratchy, chemical-finished hospital linens wrecked my own newborn's skin; everything on the shelf was either "organic"-in-name-only or a luxury markup.

**Coach:** On it. The frame, then straight to your two outputs. A personal brand is a *trust asset* — people buy from, follow, and trust a person they relate to over a faceless entity — which is exactly your diligence gap: undocumented founder trust reads as key-person risk; documented, it reads as transferable brand equity. Same IDEA pillars you ran on the product, now run on *you*. UVP needs two inputs first or it comes out generic — who you serve (audience) and what you authentically offer (you). I have most of both from your message; I'll fill the boxes, you correct.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/00-personal-brand-foundations.md⟧

**Coach:** **Insight = the intersection of audience need and your authentic offer.** Two fill-ins, done for you — push back where I'm wrong.

**Audience empathy map** (your ONE ideal follower — the new/expecting parent vetting what touches their baby's skin, not a segment in the abstract):
- **Thinks:** "'Organic' on a label means nothing; I can't tell what's actually safe."
- **Feels:** anxious, protective, burned by greenwashing, guilty about getting it wrong.
- **Says:** "it gave my baby a rash" / "is this really chemical-free or just marketing?"
- **Does:** reads certifications obsessively, screenshots ingredient lists, asks parent groups, returns anything that breaks out skin.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/01-insight-audience-and-self/00-understand-your-audience.md⟧ ⟦iv-os: read-canon (get_positioning / get_brand_voice — keep the founder voice consistent with the product brand a buyer will inherit)⟧

**Coach:** **Your self-reflection** (raw material → mission):
- *Passionate about?* what touches a newborn's skin being genuinely safe, not "organic"-in-name-only.
- *What sets you apart?* 11 years L&D nurse — you've handled more newborn skin than any founder in this category.
- *Message?* the shelf is greenwashed or overpriced; you built the real, verified, fairly-priced alternative because your own baby reacted.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/01-insight-audience-and-self/01-reflect-on-yourself.md⟧

**Maya (User):** Empathy map's right. Self-reflection — "genuinely safe" is soft. Sharpen it: I'm anti-greenwashing, certification-obsessed, almost clinical about it. The nurse part is the whole edge — that's the thing a buyer can't replicate, so document it hard. Now the mission sentence and the UVP. That's what I'm here for.

**Coach:** Sharpened: **anti-greenwashing, certification-obsessed nurse-founder.** That's your edge — and it's the load-bearing line for diligence, so it's baked in below.

**Personal mission statement** (the three compression questions — *what / who / how-you-differ* — one sentence): *"I help anxious new parents trust what touches their baby's skin by cutting through greenwashed labels with a labor-and-delivery nurse's eye for what's actually safe."*   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/01-insight-audience-and-self/01-reflect-on-yourself.md⟧

**Coach:** **Personal UVP** — your formula, filled. Formula: `I help [specific audience] achieve [specific outcome] by [unique approach]`; rule: every blank survives a "be specific" challenge — no "people," no "succeed," no "great service":

> **"I help anxious new parents put truly safe bedding and bath against their baby's skin by vetting every fabric and finish with 11 years of labor-and-delivery nursing — so they stop gambling on greenwashed labels."**

- **audience:** *anxious new parents* (not "people who want organic").
- **outcome:** *truly safe bedding/bath against their baby's skin* (the actual job — not "feel good about a purchase").
- **approach:** *11 years of L&D nursing applied to fabric + finish vetting* (distinctive method only you have — not "high-quality materials").

That clears the book's bar — its example is "small business owners / double their sales / data-driven strategies simple to implement": named audience, concrete outcome, distinctive method. Confirm and I move to the plan.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/00-your-unique-value-proposition.md⟧

**Maya (User):** UVP's locked — first version that doesn't sound like every "clean baby brand" on LinkedIn. Before the plan: give me the connective tissue so the content isn't random — how do I sound, and what do I never compromise on. And it has to read consistent enough that an operator could keep posting it after I'm out. Fast.

**Coach:** Three filters, locked before content so every post passes the same test and an operator can run it without you — not vibes:

**Signature style — three tone adjectives** (your portable filter for every post/reply — "does this sound like me?"): **clinical, plainspoken, protective.** Clinical = the certification rigor; plainspoken = anti-jargon, anti-greenwash; protective = you've *been* the scared parent, so it's not lecturing.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/01-signature-style.md⟧

**Coach:** **Speak their language** — mirror the parent, not the textile chemist. In founder posts: "rash," "chemical smell," "is this actually safe," "greenwashed" — *not* "OEKO-TEX Standard 100 Annex 6," "formaldehyde-free resin finish." Jargon is the thing you're rebelling against; don't smuggle it back in. (Run it as a literal audit on existing posts; keep the polls/Q&A loop so the captured phrasing stays current.)   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/02-speak-their-language.md⟧

**Coach:** **Non-negotiable values — capped at three** (more than three and they stop being non-negotiable). Each constrains a real decision or it's doing no work — and a buyer reads these as the brand's guardrails:
1. **Certified, not "organic"-in-name-only** — won't claim safe without third-party proof; rules out unverified "natural"/"eco" supplier marketing.
2. **Skin-safe over margin** — no chemical softeners/finishes for a cheaper feel; rules out the higher-margin conventional-finish reformulation.
3. **Honest about what it can't do** — won't claim it cures eczema; rules out medical-overreach before/after posts.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/03-authentic/00-non-negotiable-values.md⟧

**Maya (User):** Those three are the line. Voice is right. Now the 90-day plan — weight it toward stories, that's the part I have and competitors don't, and it's the part that documents the founder narrative for diligence. Give me the structure for the stories too so an operator isn't staring at a blank post.

**Coach:** Plan's built on the book's 5-step personal-brand strategy; steps 1–2 are done (one-sentence statement = your mission/UVP above; platform = LinkedIn-first, locked). The deliverable step is the **90-day content calendar — a mix of stories, educational posts, and engagement.** Built below, story-weighted per your edge.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/04-personal-brand-strategy.md⟧

**Coach:** Your stories aren't freeform — every one runs the **three-element framework**, and the third beat is the one founders skip (it's what turns a memoir into connection):
- **Challenge** you faced → **Steps** you took → **Outcome + how it relates to *their* journey.**

And at least one is the **vulnerability** post — authenticity isn't perfection. One honest post about a real failure + the lesson. Yours writes itself: *the nurse who trusted a label and put it on her own newborn anyway.* Challenge = your baby reacted to "organic" linens you'd vetted by the label alone; Steps = you stopped trusting labels, learned to read certifications, built the verified alternative; Outcome-for-them = "if a labor-and-delivery nurse got fooled by the label, it's not your fault you did too."   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/02-distinctive-and-empathetic/03-share-relatable-stories.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/03-authentic/01-show-vulnerability.md⟧

**Maya (User):** That vulnerability post is the one I've avoided for two years — it's also the asset that documents *why* this brand exists for a buyer. Fine. Drop the full 90-day calendar and I'll run it (or hand it to my operator). One next action at the end, not five.

**Coach:** Full plan below — three 30-day months, every post tagged Story / Educational / Engagement, every Story slot pre-loaded with its challenge→steps→outcome so neither you nor an operator faces a blank box. Steps 4–5 of the strategy (engage consistently, then monitor & adapt) are baked in as the weekly cadence + the day-90 review, so this is a *practice that survives handover*, not a one-time launch.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/02-personal-brand/04-personal-brand-strategy.md⟧ ⟦iv-os: write-produced-asset (log_asset → personal UVP + 90-day content plan, tagged for the diligence data-room, request_ids returned)⟧

### Artifact produced

**Personal UVP (`run_conversation`, formula filled verbatim from the Ch10 template `I help [audience] achieve [outcome] by [approach]`):**

> **"I help anxious new parents put truly safe bedding and bath against their baby's skin by vetting every fabric and finish with 11 years of labor-and-delivery nursing — so they stop gambling on greenwashed labels."**

| UVP blank | Maya's fill (survives "be specific") |
|---|---|
| **[specific audience]** | anxious new parents |
| **[specific outcome]** | truly safe bedding/bath against their baby's skin |
| **[unique approach]** | 11 years of L&D nursing applied to fabric + finish vetting (anti-greenwashing) |

**Supporting frame (filled for her, not taught):**
- **Mission (one sentence):** "I help anxious new parents trust what touches their baby's skin by cutting through greenwashed labels with a labor-and-delivery nurse's eye for what's actually safe."
- **Audience empathy map (Thinks/Feels/Says/Does):** "'organic' means nothing" skeptic · anxious + protective + burned by greenwashing · "it gave my baby a rash" / "is this actually chemical-free?" · reads certs, screenshots labels, returns anything that breaks out skin.
- **Signature tone (3 adjectives):** clinical · plainspoken · protective.
- **Speak-their-language rule:** "rash," "chemical smell," "is this actually safe," "greenwashed" — never "OEKO-TEX Annex 6"/"formaldehyde-free resin finish."
- **Non-negotiable values (capped at 3, each constrains a real decision):** Certified, not "organic"-in-name-only · Skin-safe over margin · Honest about what it can't do.

**90-Day Content Plan (`run_conversation`, Ch10 5-step strategy → the 90-day calendar deliverable; story-weighted; Story slots pre-loaded with challenge→steps→outcome; built to be operator-runnable for handover):**

**Month 1 — Show up & establish the edge** (cadence: 3 posts/week, engage daily)
- *Story* — **The vulnerability post (anchor):** Challenge: an L&D nurse who trusted an "organic" label and her own newborn reacted → Steps: stopped trusting labels, learned to read certifications, built the verified alternative → Outcome-for-them: "if a labor-and-delivery nurse got fooled by the label, it's not your fault you did too."
- *Educational* — "'Organic' vs. actually certified — what the label is and isn't telling you" (mirror language: *is this really safe?*, not the jargon).
- *Engagement* — Poll: "What made you return a baby product you'd bought?" (listen + capture their words).

**Month 2 — Build authority on the evidence** (same cadence; reply to every comment)
- *Story* — **The turning-point story:** Challenge: a parent whose baby "reacted to everything" → Steps: walked her through reading a textile cert like a nurse reads a chart → Outcome-for-them: her baby stopped breaking out and she stopped second-guessing every purchase.
- *Educational* — "How to read a baby-fabric certification in 60 seconds" + "Why a chemical smell on new linens isn't 'normal'" (clinical, plainspoken, protective).
- *Engagement* — Q&A: "Send me a label, I'll tell you what's actually in it" (the listening loop).

**Month 3 — Convert trust to loyalty** (same cadence)
- *Story* — **The mission story:** Challenge: the shelf is greenwashed or a luxury markup → Steps: built the verified, fairly-priced alternative → Outcome-for-them: "you don't have to choose between actually-safe and affordable."
- *Educational* — "3 claims baby-textile brands make that the certification doesn't back" (values: certified-not-in-name-only, honest about limits).
- *Engagement* — Reply-to-DMs + featured-parent stories; soft point to B0HOM0003 for those who ask.

**Day 90 — Monitor & adapt:** pull LinkedIn analytics, double down on the post type with the most saves/comments, retire the weakest, re-set the next 90 — and snapshot the published founder narrative into the diligence data-room as the documented, transferable trust asset.

**One next action:** post the vulnerability story this week — it's your highest-trust, lowest-competition asset, it unblocks the whole calendar, and it's the founder-narrative artifact the broker needs documented for the multiple.

- [schema] Each `run_conversation` call returns one assembled Trevor reply per turn (streamed `text_delta`/`extracted_fields` buffered into a single message), progressively populating the personal-brand fields (empathy map, mission, UVP blanks, tone adjectives, values, content calendar); no per-token streaming surfaced to the user.
- [skill-faithful] "personal brand is a trust asset — people buy from/follow/trust a person they relate to over a faceless entity; same IDEA pillars applied to a person" traces to 00-personal-brand-foundations.md; the Thinks/Feels/Says/Does empathy map for ONE ideal person traces to 01-insight-audience-and-self/00-understand-your-audience.md; the self-reflection questions (passionate/sets-apart/message) + the mission three (what/who/how-differ) + "insight = intersection of audience need and authentic offer" trace to 01-insight-audience-and-self/01-reflect-on-yourself.md; the UVP formula `I help [audience] achieve [outcome] by [approach]` + the "be specific / no generic blanks" rule + the book's small-business-owners example as the bar trace to 02-distinctive-and-empathetic/00-your-unique-value-proposition.md; the three-tone-adjectives portable filter traces to 02-distinctive-and-empathetic/01-signature-style.md; "mirror how the audience talks, drop the jargon" + the literal post-copy audit + the polls/Q&A listening loop trace to 02-distinctive-and-empathetic/02-speak-their-language.md; the three-element story framework (challenge → steps → outcome-and-how-it-relates-to-their-journey, with the third beat enforced) traces to 02-distinctive-and-empathetic/03-share-relatable-stories.md; "cap values at three, each must constrain a real decision (sustainability-rules-out example)" traces to 03-authentic/00-non-negotiable-values.md; "authenticity ≠ perfection; one honest post about a real failure + the lesson" traces to 03-authentic/01-show-vulnerability.md; the 5-step strategy (reflect → platforms → 90-day content calendar → engage consistently → monitor & adapt) with the 90-day calendar as the one concrete deliverable traces to 04-personal-brand-strategy.md.
- [persona-adapt:P1] Coach led with the answer + one-line why and explicitly skipped teaching the IDEA pillars she said she knows; compressed; filled every box FOR her from her own data (empathy map, mission, UVP, tone, values) and invited correction rather than handing her a blank worksheet; produced a done-for-you paste-ready personal UVP and a fully pre-loaded 90-day calendar (Story slots already populated with challenge→steps→outcome) she edits or hands to an operator; used HER data (B0HOM0003, ~12-month exit, broker key-person/brand-risk flag, 11-years L&D nurse, own newborn's reaction, anti-greenwashing stance); batched questions; ended on exactly one next action. Compression + done-for-you draft both present.
- [artifact] Both promised deliverables present: a personal UVP with all three formula blanks filled and specificity-checked, plus a 90-day content plan (three months, every post tagged Story/Educational/Engagement, Story slots pre-structured, day-90 monitor-and-adapt review with the diligence data-room snapshot).
- [safety] No cross-tenant data; all inputs are Maya's own (ASIN, exit timeline, broker note, founder background, anti-greenwashing stance) or tool outputs; IV-OS canon read and asset write shown only as ⟦iv-os:⟧ capability tags, never invented as concrete records; no fabricated stats, certifications, clinical outcomes, or valuation multiples beyond the cited Ch10 skills — the UVP/mission/values/stories are all built from Maya-supplied material, and the values block explicitly bars unverified "organic"/"natural" claims and medical-overreach (eczema-cure) posts.
